entry.title: Supply Chain Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition 2023
entry.content:
SAP has released the 2023 release (Feature Pack Stack FPS0) of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition on 11th October 2023.

This blog post provides an overview of innovations for Supply Chain Management.

For a focussed start, check out the next video from m.oertelt highlighting the following innovations:

  • Advanced Shipping and Receiving for Decentralized EWM and TM

  • Warehouse Management - Advanced Shipping and Receiving Cockpit

  • Transportation Management - Calculation of Greenhouse Gas Emissions

  • Inventory Management - Composite Barcode Scanning




Video 1: Highlights for Supply Chain Management in the 2023 release

 

Table of content (for quick access, just click on one of the business area links):


  • Advanced Intercompany Processes




  • Advanced Available-to-Promise (aATP)




  • Handling Unit Management




  • Process Flow Transparency




  • Inventory Management




  • Extended Warehouse Management




  • Transportation Management




  • Kanban




  • Delivery




  • Extended Service Parts Planning




 

Advanced Intercompany Processes


First, we launched the Advanced Intercompany Processes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition with the 2208.3 release. Since this time, the blog post

  • Advanced Intercompany Sales & Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA Cloud


has attracted particularly great interest.
Advanced intercompany business processes include Advanced Intercompany Sales and Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer.
With the 2022 release, we made Advanced Intercompany Processes also available in SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, see the blog post

  • Highlights for Supply Chain in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition and SAP S/4HANA | 2022 Release.


What's new for Adv. ICo with the 2023 release?

Configure Posting Date for Advanced Intercompany Invoices


In Customizing for Materials Management under Advanced Intercompany Business Processes the new Customizing activity Configure Posting Date for Advanced Intercompany Invoices is available: You can specify for each individual invoicing party which date is to be used for the posting date of the intercompany supplier invoice:

  • If you leave the Invoicing Party field blank but specify a value for the posting date, this posting date is used for all invoicing parties that are not explicitly specified.

  • If neither a specific entry nor a default entry exists for an invoicing party, the billing date from the referenced billing document is taken as the standard behavior.



Figure 1: New configuration step 'Configure Posting Date for Advanced Intercompany Invoices'

 

Deactivation of Advanced Intercompany Sales


You can deactivate the Advanced Intercompany Sales process (scope item 5D2), for example, if you want to switch to the classic intercompany sales process.

For this purpose, you can use the business add-in (BAdI) BADI_SDSLS_VCM_PROC_DEACTIVATE (Deactivation of Advanced Intercompany Sales) to define that an intercompany sales order item should not be processed as an advanced intercompany sales order item.
If you deactivate the Advanced Intercompany Sales process, you can switch to the classic intercompany sales process.

 

(back to top)

 

Advanced Available-to-Promise (aATP)


sujeet.acharya summarizes the aATP innovations in his blog post

  • 2023 Release Highlights of SAP S/4HANA advanced Available-To-Promise (aATP)


In my past blog post

  • Highlights for Supply Chain in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition and SAP S/4HANA | 2022 Release


you will find a list of further blog posts about

  • Supply Creation-Based Confirmation (SBC),

  • Supply Protection

  • Product Allocation

  • Backorder Processing


In addition, I want to draw your attention to two new blog posts:

  • How advanced ATP can rescue your next party

  • Supply Protection on the Level of the Storage Location


 

(back to top)

 

Handling Unit Management


Customer Defined Packing and Picking


With the new app Manage Handling Units in Production – Scheduling Agreement, the user with the role of Production Supervisor - Repetitive Manufacturing can create planned handling units for sales scheduling agreement items in the repetitive manufacturing context.

Figure 2: Fiori app ‘Manage Handling Units in Production – Scheduling Agreement’ (F6445)

The app is one step in an end-to-end process, called Customer defined Packing and Picking, including new functionality in EWM.

It is the successor of the old transaction MFPP (Create Planned Handling Units) but only for scheduling agreements.

(back to top)

 

Handling Unit Situation


For some products shipped with handling units it is critical, that during the delivery certain basic conditions are met, for example, regarding the temperature, the humidity, or vibrations. To monitor these handling units, you can attach sensors to the handling unit before it leaves your warehouse to make sure that you get informed when defined thresholds are exceeded. The sensors send their data to a third party Internet of Things (IoT) system, which then creates a situation using the Handling Unit API to inform the shipping specialist. The person receives this notification, which leads to the relevant entry in the My Situations app. In the app, the shipping specialist can see, for example, which handling unit is affected and what the sensor detected. You can adapt the displayed texts to your own business purposes.

Let's take a closer look how the Handling Unit Situation scenario is technically realized:

ODATA API: Handling Unit


You can now use the MoveHandlingUnits operation of the ODATA API Handling Unit to perform good movement postings for handling units for additional business scenarios. The next figure shows the capabilities of the API for movement from a non-HU-managed storage location:


Figure 3: ODATA API Handling Unit - Movement from non-HU-managed storage location

With the new CreateSituation operation, you can now create situations for handling units managed in storage locations in Logistics (LO) as well as in Warehouse Management (WM). This new operation can be used by third party Internet of Things (IoT) systems, which collect information from connected sensors attached to handling units.


Figure 4: Handling Unit Situation - Architecture


Situation Template: Handling Unit Situation


Use the new standard template for Handling Unit Situation if you want your users to be informed instantly when the measurement of a sensor attached to a handling unit exceeds its defined threshold. The user gets a notification, which leads to the relevant entry in the My Situations app.



Figure 5: 'Manage Situation Types' app - Use new situation template 'Handling Unit Situation' instead of deprecated situation template 'Handling Unit IOT'

The next figure shows the details of the new situation template in the Manage Situation Types app.


Figure 6: 'Manage Situation Types' app - Detail page of new template 'Handling Unit Situation'

For this new situation template, the information concerning critical situations of the handling unit, for example, the temperature threshold is exceeded, must be sent to a third party Internet of Things (IoT) system, which then creates the situation using the Handling Unit API. For more information, see CreateSituation.

The next figure shows how the user will be informed about the situation in the app Monitor Handling Units. I recorded the screenshot from a test scenario in a SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition system. But consider that the app Monitor Handling Units is only available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition.

As mentioned above, in SAP S/4HANA, you can see the situation in the app My Situations.


Figure 7: 'Monitor Handling Units' app - Situation notification informs the user that the threshold of 40 CEL was exceed and the current temperature of the handling unit is 77 CEL.

For further information, see Handling Unit Situation.

 

(back to top)

 

Process Flow Transparency


Are you using a Web shop app that is connected to an SAP S/4HANA backend system? And do you want to efficiently monitor the logistics processes of your sales orders within SAP S/4HANA?

The solution for you is Process Flow Transparency.


For a short introduction in Process Flow Transparency read my past blog post

  • Highlights for Supply Chain in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition and SAP S/4HANA | 2022 Release


Check out the brand new blog post

  • Keeping Track of Logistics Activities with Process Flow Transparency


from simon.liebel in which he demonstrates a practical use case scenario to show how Process Flow Transparency can benefit your business.

 

(back to top)

 

Inventory Management


Composite Barcode Scanning


Barcode scanning is widely used for various purposes due to its efficiency and accuracy in capturing and processing information. Barcodes provide a unique identifier for each product. Scanning barcodes eliminates the need for manual data entry, which can be time-consuming and prone to errors. Barcodes facilitate efficient inventory management by enabling real-time tracking of products entering and leaving a warehouse or store. Scanning barcodes during receiving, picking, and shipping operations helps maintain accurate stock levels, reduce stockouts, and optimize reordering.

In recent releases, we have already enabled barcode scanning in many Inventory Management apps (see my blog posts for the releases 2021, and 2022).

The next figure describes the difference between scanning of simple or composite barcodes in SAP S/4HANA Inventory Management:



Figure 8: Barcode scanning in SAP S/4HANA Inventory Management

Now, the possibility to handle composite barcodes has been added to the barcode scanning function in the apps:

  • Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document

  • Post Goods Receipt for Production Order

  • Post Goods Receipt for Process Order

  • Post Goods Receipt for Inbound Delivery

  • Post Subsequent Adjustment

  • Stock - Single Material

  • Manage Stock

  • Transfer Stock - In-Plant


Such barcodes are based on the international GS1 standard (see GS1 General Specifications Standard) that allows defining multiple information in one barcode. The next figure shows a composite barcode that e.g. contains GTIN, Packaging Date, and Batch information.


Figure 9: Composite Barcode

Please note that industry is moving toward two-dimensional (2D) barcodes. Check out the GS1 announcement Get Ready for Sunrise 2027. All linear barcodes will be replaced with GS1 QR codes by 2027. SAP S/4HANA Inventory Management is ready for the change.

Therefore, a new central configuration setting has also been created for this purpose. For more information, see Barcode Scanning in Inventory Management.

The next figure summarizes the customizing steps for barcode scanning in Inventory Management.



Figure 10: Customizing for barcode scanning in Inventory Management

Check out the next demo video in which Stefanie Bierbach, Product Owner for Inventory Management shows the composite barcode scanning in the Manage Stock and Transfer Stock - In Plant apps.



Video 2: 'Manage Stock' app - Composite Barcode scanning + Mass processing via 'Item List'

In addition, the demo video above shows the new Add To List action and Item List section that is described in the next chapter.

(back to top)

 

Manage Stock - Mass posting with Item List for stock mass posting


The new Add To List action and Item List section enables mass processing for the apps:

  • Manage Stock

  • Transfer Stock – In-Plant


You can post more than one material at the same time if you first temporarily save the selected materials in an item list (Add To List action). From this item list, you can later select the materials to be posted at once.


Figure 11: 'Manage Stock' app - 'Add to List' action for mass posting on 'Item List' section

(back to top)

 

Manage Stock


Enhancements to Manage Stock app


You can now use the goods issue for cost center (201 postings) and special stock type K (supplier consignment) in the Manage Stock app.

If you use serial numbers, you now have the flexibility to create new serial numbers for a storage location while transferring or scrapping materials even if you have a stock check warning or no stock check selected in your serial number profile.


Figure 12: 'Manage Stock' app enhancements - use for movement type 201, special stock type K, create new serial number while transferring or scrapping materials

Usability Improvements


The Stock – Single Material App is now enhanced to provide filter options for fields like plant, batch. This helps customer to find and load preferred plants.


Figure 13: 'Stock - Single Material' app - new filter options for fields like plant and batch

Further, you can also now have additional settings to exclude batches with deletion flag, exclude batches with zero stock in the Transfer Stock – Cross Plant app.


Figure 14: 'Transfer Stock - Cross Plant' app - new view settings to exclude empty batches and batches with deletion flag

(back to top)

 

Analyze Stock in Date Range and Schedule Export for Inventory Analytics


With the 2022 release, we launched the SAP Fiori app Analyze Stock in Date Range that enables you to analyze and monitor goods movement postings for one or more materials in a defined date range (see my 2022 blog post ).

In the app, you can now do the following:
The job scheduling function has been enhanced to start an export report to Microsoft Excel with the
inventory analysis results. The new button Schedule Export has been introduced for direct access. Alternatively, you can open the app Schedule Export of Inventory Analytics from the Fiori launchpad as show in the figure below. With this app, you can schedule a background job to export material stock and posting data for a defined time period. Since the retrieval of large queries can be time-consuming, SAP recommends that you move such queries to resource-uncritical phases of the day.


Figure 15: Open 'Analyze Stock in Date Range' app (F6185) and 'Schedule Export of Inventory Analytics' app (F7493) from Fiori launchpad or use button 'Schedule Export' for direct job scheduling

The new button Schedule Export for direct access to job scheduling is available in both the main page and the detail page of the Analyze Stock in Date Range app.


Figure 16: 'Analyze Stock in Date Range' app - Detail page with button 'Schedule Export'

Check out the SAP Help Portal in case of any questions about the Analyze Stock in Date Range app:

  • Analyze Stock in Date Range | SAP Help Portal

  • Details for Calculating and Displaying Values | SAP Help Portal


(back to top)

 

Physical Inventory


Create Physical Inventory Documents


The usability of the Create Physical Inventory Documents app has been improved:

  • A new filter allows you to select either materials with or without movement. Now, you can also use this app to create physical inventory documents for materials for which no material documents exist.

  • You can now schedule a background job that processes the creation of physical inventory documents. In addition, you can combine this creation with a print option using the same background job.



Figure 17: Schedule Physical Inventory Document Creation

 

Manage Physical Inventory Documents - Post all items at once


The posting function in the Manage Physical Inventory Documents app has been enhanced. You can now make mass postings more conveniently and more quickly. Instead of selecting individual items of a physical inventory document, you can now select and post all items of a physical inventory document at once. Before posting for a complete physical inventory document, you can specify in a dialog the reason for each item difference type (increase/decrease). The possible reasons are displayed in the value help.


Figure 18: 'Manage Physical Inventory Documents' app - post all items of a physical inventory document at once

(back to top)

 

Label Printing in Goods Receipts


You can now you can print multiple goods receipt slips and/or labels when posting a goods receipt in the following apps:

  • Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document

  • Post Goods Receipt for Production Order

  • Post Goods Receipt for Inbound Delivery

  • Post Goods Receipt for Process Order

  • Post Goods Receipt without Reference


In the newly added Number of Labels field, you can manually enter the number of labels to print or let the system calculate the number of printed labels for you. In both cases, you must first maintain the Label type and Lab. Form fields in the material master record under the plant data view.


Figure 19: 'Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document' app - new field 'Number of GR Slips' on GR item object page

(back to top)

 

Consignment Stock Reporting


With the Consignment Stock Reporting apps, you can keep an overview of goods movements going in and out of your consignment stock and set up an automated email notification to suppliers about the current state of their stock. This process helps expedite stock replenishment and provides stock transparency.

Consignment Stock Reporting includes 3 apps:

  • Manage Stock Reporting Procedures

  • Manage Stock Reporting Subscriptions

  • Stock Reporting Overview


For detailed information how to set up the scenario, check out the SAP Help.

The following figures show the three new apps:


Figure 20: 'Manage Stock Reporting Procedures' app


Figure 21: 'Manage Stock Reporting Subscription' app - Object Page


Figure 22: 'Stock Reporting Overview' app


Figure 23: 'Stock Reporting Overview' app - Object Page

 

(back to top)

 

Extended Warehouse Management


florian.kuchta2 gives an comprehensive overview of innovations and new features in SAP Extended Warehouse Management. I encourage you to read his blog posts:

  • SAP® Extended Warehouse Management in SAP S/4HANA 2023 – What’s New

  • SAP Extended Warehouse Management in SAP S/4HANA 2022 FPS01 – What’s New


 

(back to top)

 

Transportation Management


eric.osterroth gives an comprehensive overview of innovations and new features in SAP Transportation Management. I encourage you to read his blog posts:

  • Highlights of Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA 2023 Release and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition

  • SAP Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA 2022 – FPS01 – What’s new?


 

(back to top)

 

Kanban


Kanban – Manage Production Supply Areas


The new SAP Fiori app Manage Production Supply Areas (F6935) helps you to manage your production supply areas and enables you to create, edit, and delete production supply areas. You can personalize the displayed list of production supply areas according to your use cases. You can navigate to further details including in which contexts production supply areas are being used.


Figure 24: SAP Fiori app 'Manage Production Supply Areas' - Create, edit, and delete functionality

(back to top)

 

Kanban Container Status


Define Kanban Container Status Texts


With this feature you can define container status texts in different languages. The maximum length for the container status text has increased to 12 characters. You can overwrite the predefined container status texts, to improve usability and understanding of Kanban processes in your environment.


Figure 25: Configuration of own status text

Define Kanban Container Status Sequences


With this feature you can define kanban container status sequences, and permissions or restrictions for container status changes. In the control cycle you can select one of your status sequences in preference to a predefined status sequence.


Figure 26: Definition of own status sequence

(back to top)

 

Kanban Stock Transfer with Warehouse Requests for Embedded EWM


A new kanban replenishment strategy is available for embedded EWM stock transfer using warehouse requests.

So two stock transfer possibilities are now available on a decentral EWM system:

  • Stock transfer with warehouse task

  • Stock transfer with warehouse request (new)



Figure 27: Stock transfer with warehouse request on decentral EWM - System architecture

 


Figure 28: Stock transfer with warehouse request on decentral EWM

 

You can choose the new control type Stock Transfer with Warehouse Request in the configuration activity Kanban -> Replenishment Strategies -> Define Stock Transfer Strategies.

For further information, see Replenishment Strategies for Stock Transfer.

 

(back to top)

 

Delivery


Enable Inbound Delivery Processing for Serialized Subcontracting Components


You can now enter and display serial numbers for subcontracting components in the Change Inbound Delivery (VL32N) app.

In addition, the following services are extended and supporting now serial numbers for subcontracting components in inbound deliveries:

  • SOAP API: Inbound Delivery - Create (B2B) (DELIVERYREQUEST_IN)

  • Odata API: Inbound Delivery (A2X) (API_INBOUND_DELIVERY_SRV_0002)



Figure 29: Odata API Inbound Delivery (A2X) - serial numbers are now supported for subcontracting components

Prerequisite: In order to use serial numbers for subcontracting components in inbound deliveries, the component must have a serial number profile assigned at the time of the inbound delivery.

(back to top)

 

Further integration enhancements to Advanced Variant Configuration


Now you can navigate from the Delivery Fact Sheet to the configuration values in the AVC UI. In addition, you can print the configuration values of the delivery items on Delivery Notes.


Figure 30: Navigation from Delivery Fact Sheet to the configuration values in the AVC UI

 

(back to top)

 

Extended Service Parts Planning (eSPP)


Forecasting


Multiple external time series in Forecasting


Let us delve into an example showing the relationship between Demand History and Temperature.



Figure 31: Example showing the relationship between Demand History and Temperature

You can now

  • Maintain one or multiple external time series that capture the behavior of an external factor (e.g. temperature, gross domestic product, installed base of engines etc.) over time.

  • Use external time series in forecasting (currently limited to forecast models of Gradient Boosting and ARIMAX) as an independent variable.

  • View external time series alongside location / product key figures within the Interactive Forecast UI.


and

  • Planning levels supported for external time series include Product and Location, Product only, Location only, Region, or Product Group.


The major benefit of using external time series is the ability that forecast models can account for the potential impact of external factors on your forecasts. This results in a higher forecast accuracy, leading to more informed decision-making and strategic planning.

The next 3 figures show the customizing steps to Maintain Time Series Profile and Assign key figures to External Time Series Profile:


Figure 32: Step 1 - Transaction SPRO "Define Profile for External Time Series"


Figure 33: Step 2 - Transaction SPRO "Define Forecast Service Profile” - Link TS ID in Forecast Service Profile


Figure 34: Step 3 - Transaction /SAPAPO/SPPFCSTPRF - Link TS ID in Forecast Profile

 

Forecast with ARIMAX Model


Make use of the intelligent ARIMAX forecast model to enhance planning quality. You can now

  • Leverage the autoregressive integrated moving average algorithm with additional external data (ARIMAX) in eSPP

  • Forecast the need for spare parts in upcoming periods based on past values and include additional key figures

  • Use automatic model selection to determine if ARIMA, ARIMAX or any other Forecast model is a best fit



Figure 35: Forecast with ARIMAX Model 

 

Gradient boosting of decision trees in Forecasting


Now we come to artificial intelligence! Use gradient boosting of decision trees forecast with multiple external time series to enhance planning quality. This feature offers the following capabilities:

  • The ability to upload an external time series and the provision of the gradient boosting of decision trees model allow you to use a machine-learning forecast that incorporates external data to generate a better forecast.

  • This scope item will provide an improvement of the user experience and allow to utilize multiple external time series in eSPP (Extended Service parts) forecasting.

  • Along with historical data and forecast figures, users can display multiple external time series in the interactive forecasting UI.



Figure 35: Gradient boosting of decision trees in Forecasting

(back to top)

 

Distribution Requirements Planning (DRP) - Repair or Buy


With the Repair or Buy functionality you can plan the repair of already used, broken and returned (unserviceable) parts. Using this feature, eSPP Distribution Requirements Planning (DRP) runs result in planned refurbishment orders in case of an in-house repair or in purchase requisitions in case subcontractors are involved. Parts of the required demand may also be replenished via external procurement instead of repair, as usual in eSPP. In many cases the repair of parts is more cost-efficient than the procurement of completely new parts.


Figure 36: Use repair-or-buy functionality to gain planning accuracy by covering demand using repairable products

 

In addition, eSPP offers many new features also in the area of Capture Demand, Inventory Planning, and Cross-functional topics that are described in the What's New document. For more information watch the recording of the eSPP session during our last product expert training (see link in the next chapter).

 

(back to top)

 

More Information / Additional Links


Thanks for reading this blog post. Stay tuned!

If you are also interested in other Lines of Business and Industries for this new SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition 2023 release, I would like to draw your attention to the blog posts

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition | 2023 Release from christian.vogler2

  • 2023 Release Highlights in Seconds: SAP S/4HANA & SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition | SAP Blogs from noboru.ota.


If you want to see the entire PDF presentation of all innovations, see here.

If you want to learn more about SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, join our Partner Community. This group focuses on providing enhanced learnings and periodic updates with respect to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition product. Our goal is to facilitate collaboration with your peers and SAP subject matter experts.

For general roadmap information please check the SAP S/4HANA Roadmap.

If want to learn more about the described innovations watch the recording of the last Product Expert Training that you will find on our landing page (see Sessions in Week 2 -> Plan to Fulfill track).

  • Landing Page for SAP Partners

  • Landing Page for SAP Colleagues


Further Information:



  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Manufacturing & Supply Chain – The Link Collection

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition release info: com/s4hana

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, and SAP S/4HANA Community here

  • SAP S/4HANA PSCC Digital Enablement Wheel here

  • Inside SAP S/4HANA Podcast here

  • Best practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition here

  • Help Portal Product Page here

  • Feature Scope Description here

  • What’s New here


Follow us via @SAP and #S4HANA, or myself via @GerhardWelker and LinkedIn

entry.title: Data Transfer to GBQ From S/4HANA Private Cloud
entry.content:
In SAP Implementation one of the most critical aspects is reporting. As SAP data is being integrated with many other Non SAP system’s to create reports in systems such as looker, this blog explores the option of how SAP can be Integrated to GBQ using GBQ Connector.

This blog explores the option of data transfer from SAP to GBQ using embedded SLT in S/4HANA.

1        System Considerations:

  • S/4HANA Rise Private Edition
  • S/4HANA Embedded SLT
  • Google Big Query

2        Scenario

As part of this blog, we will be setting up data transmission from SAP S/4HANA system hosted in GCP to GBQ using GBQ Connector and embedded SLT.

3        Infrastructure Setup between SAP and GBQ

The official guide from Google for the setup is available over here. (https://cloud.google.com/solutions/sap/docs/bq-connector/latest/all-guides )

The infrastructure setup can be divided into three part, depending on where the SAP system is hosted. This use case considers S/4HANA Private cloud   in Compute Engine virtual machine (VM) on Google Cloud.

3.1      SAP Application server Infrastructure setup

  • Install gcloud CLI in S4HANA OS :If not already available , install gcloud CLI in OS of application server of SAP and verify that it is installed using command gcloud -v

  • Cloud API access scopes for Application VM of SAP : This can be provided in two way, one being full access to all API and the other is  only provide Big Query and Cloud Platform API access. This access should be enabled in all application server of SAP system .

  • Enable the host VM to obtain access tokens : The below permission should be provided to the service account associated with the VM of SAP application server.

Service Account Token Creator, BigQuery Data Editor , BigQuery Job User

  • Enable firewall to allow access to Google Cloud APIs : Allow access to API (https://bigquery.googleapis.com ,https://iamcredentials.googleapis.com ) which will be used for RFC connection between SAP and GBQ.

3.2      GCP Infrastructure Setup

As this use case considered SAP Rise system is hosted in GCP, we have 2 steps to perform in the customer’s GCP environment to allow

Data transfer.

  • Create a Big Query Dataset in GBQ: As this process varies across organizations , please create a Big Query Dataset in your environment and take a note of the project name and dataset name . This information will be later used for the SLT configuration.
  • Allow SAP VM Service Account access in Customer GCP: In this use case GBQ Dataset will be created is separate project from SAP VM. The GCP admin where the GBQ dataset was created should provide access to Service account of SAP VM so it can write data into the dataset.

4        Configuration Setup

As the guide covers detailed instructions to be performed, this blog will demonstrate highlights of the configuration in SAP. Feel free to ask any questions in the comment section of the blog for additional information.

  • GBQ Connector download and Installation: Download ( https://bq-slt-connector.appspot.com/bqconnectorforsap)  the GBQ connector transport package using billing account associated with Organization’s GCP.  Install the package using STMS .

  • Set up SSL certificates in STRUST : Download the relevant root certificate (GTS Root R1 ,GTS CA 1C3)  for GCP and import into SSL Client PSE of SAP.
  • Specify access settings in /GOOG/CLIENT_KEY:  The below values are considered in this setup.

Service Account Name : The Service Account associated with SAP Application Server VM is used

Scope: https://www.googleapis.com/auth/bigquery can be used if the permission were restricted in earlier step

Project ID : The project name created is target GCP which contains the GBQ dataset

  • Create the RFC in SM59 : Create 2 Type G RFC for GCP API and One Type 3 for SAP RFC (Own system ) for data transfer

  • Create SLT Configuration using LTRC

  • Configure /GOOG/SLT_SETTINGS : configure a mass transfer for BigQuery and specify the table and field mappings.

Project Identifier: This is the project name from Target GCP

BQ Dataset: Name of the GBQ Dataset

5        Test Replication:

After the above setup, test the replication using LTRC. All the other relevant configurations for SLT can be used in the above use case as well (LTRS) .

6        Considerations

  • This setup is intended for replication where the data transfer is not in large scale as there will be performance impact to SAP system for embedded SLT scenario.
  • Check with SAP ECS team regarding the security approval required for the changes to the SAP VM access.
  • Advance performance replication for certain larger table such as ACDOCA should be considered using Performance Optimization Guide for SLT
  • As the use case had considered embedded SLT, certain performance optimization option will increase the database table size for temporary logging table.
  • Periodically program CNV_NOTE_ANALYZER_SLT to check for latest available SAP notes which can be applied to improve and fix any bugs associated with SLT.

Resources

https://cloud.google.com/solutions/sap/docs/bq-connector/latest/install-config-on-gc#prereqs

https://help.sap.com/doc/04989b617c6f4ca8982fde703e7350f9/3.0.04/en-US/Performance%20Guide.pdf

2014562 - FAQ: SAP HANA LT Replication Server (SLT)

2940799 - Best practices for loading/replicating a table with a view – SLT

3016862 - DMIS Note Analyzers with separated scenarios for ABAP-based Migration and Replication Technology

Please keep an eye out upcoming blog about lessons learnt as well as optimization option we have implemented for above use case.

entry.title: MDG DQM: Troubleshooting DQM validation rules for central governance - Product Master S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Hi All,

Season's greetings!! 

In 2023 I focussed on sharing my insights on the data quality management module of SAP Master Data Governance through multiple blogs( "as if it was used be called in the old website"). If you have missed some of these then here is a quick link to all of the content published earlier.

  1.  MDG DQM: Enriching product master data with derivation rules in S/4 HANA
  2.  MDG DQM:Employing rules in central governance & mass processing in S/4 HANA
  3.  MDG DQM: Empowering Your Business with High-Quality Data in S/4HANA

As I start 2024, I have delved a little deeper into this topic and with the new community platform launched, I was excited to share some of the insights from SAP Master Data Governance data quality management on this new platform.

So without further Adieu let's dive in !!

INTRODUCTION

In this blog post we will cover a few steps on how we can troubleshoot the BRF+ validations which is the underlying framework for MDG DQM. This module of MDG is still evolving rapidly as we move towards cloud first and a clean core approach. As MDG developers we are still pretty comfortable with classical BADI's for doing validations and we all know it's quite easy to troubleshoot the rules. But when it comes to DQM we might wonder how do my rules get called? how can i troubleshoot my rules if they are not working as expected? 

So let's see how we can troubleshoot the rules.

MAIN CONTENT

  1.  DQM rules will be triggered when we click the check button on our MDG UI.
  2.  Once click on check the MDQ adapter CL_MDQ_COMMON_ADAPTER_PROD class will be called which will in turn call the MDQ rule engine class CL_MDQ_RULE_ENGINE_CHECK.
  3.  This rule engine check method will internally call many other methods and one of the methods which gets called is CL_MDQ_BRFPLUS_HANDLER, this class is responsible for executing the BRF+ functions by calling other standard methods. In this class, we can put a breakpoint in the execute_function method.                                     
  4. Inside execute_function we will find an FDT method which processes the BRF+ function.
  5.  Inside the process method, we will get the class name associated with the BRF function ID.        
  6. Using this class name we will call the process_dynamic method of the FDT.
  7.  Inside this method look for the process_pure method which will have the entire BRF+ generated code for a rule which you have created in DQM.                    

By following these steps you can easily navigate through the DQM validation rule framework troubleshoot your rules and make sure they are working perfectly fine.

Scenarios which need troubleshooting:

1. You import your rule from one system to another, the rule is approved and enabled for check-in change requests but still doesn't work.

2. You modified your BRF+ logic as per your requirements after the initial build, however, the latest rules don't reflect this.

System details:

S/4 HANA 2023 FPS00.

CONCLUSION

By following the above steps you will be easily able to troubleshoot DQM rules for SAP Master Data Governance central governance.

Hope you find this information useful.

Regards

Anirban

entry.title: Deep Dive - SAP Payroll Lösungen für die Schweiz
entry.content:
In zweiten Teil unserer dreiteiligen Serie zur SAP Payroll beschäftigen sich mein Kollege @RolandChristen  und ich uns mit den verschiedenen Payroll Angeboten die sich Schweizer SAP Kunden bieten. Dabei gehen wir sowohl auf den Scope insbesondere Schweizspezifika als auch auf mögliche Migrationspfade ein.

Für einen Überblick zu SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll empfehlen wir den ersten Teil unserer Serie: Lohnabrechnung mit SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll für Schweizer Kunden
 

Welche ‘Versionen’ der Payroll bietet SAP an?

Neben der bisherigen SAP HCM On Premise Payroll (welche bald verschwinden wird) bietet SAP zwei Lösungen an:

Welche ‘Versionen’ der Payroll bietet SAP an?

Innovation mit der Cloud Lösung für Human Experience Management mit SAP SuccessFactors:

  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll

  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll als Partner Managed Cloud ideal geeignet für den Mittelstand


Kontinuität mit SAP HCM For S/4 HANA:

  • SAP HCM For SAP S/4 HANA On-Premise Edition

  • SAP HCM For SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud  (Rise with SAP for HXM) (zusammen mit S/4 HANA oder separate)

Was haben die beiden ‘SAP HCM For SAP S/4 HANA’-Versionen gemeinsam und wo unterscheiden sie sich?

Gemeinsam:

  • Sie benötigen die Komponente ‘Core HR’ in SAP HCM For S/4 HANA

  • Basieren auf SAP ERP HCM (EhP 8 ) mit zusätzlichen SAP HANA-Optimierungen

  • Der funktionale Umfang ist vergleichbar mit SAP ERP HCM - mit einigen Ausnahmen (Details finden sie in diesem Knowledge Base Article: 3091160 - SAP HCM for SAP S/4HANA – General Approach and Strategy - SAP for Me)

  • Gegenüber SAP HCM On Premise Payroll gibt es keine grösseren neuen Funktionalitäten oder eine Rearchitektur

  • Folgen der SAP S/4HANA Wartungsstrategie bis 2040

  • Haben eine modernisierte, auf Fiori basierende Oberfläche 


Unterschied:

  • Bei der On-Premise Version könnten die alten Talent Prozesse aus dem SAP HCM beibehalten werden. Recruiting und Learning können als zusätzliche Lizenzen hinzugefügt werden.

  • In der Private Cloud Version von SAP HCM For S/4 HANA sind Talentprozesse nicht möglich. Dafür ist die Cloud Lösung SAP SuccessFactors vorgesehen.

Was ist die SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (SF ECP) und worin unterscheidet sie sich von der SAP HCM On Premise Lohnabrechnung?

Sehr einfach und in einem Satz gesagt: Die SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll nutzt die gleiche Abrechnungsengine wie SAP HCM (und SAP HCM for SAP S/4HANA), jedoch als von SAP gehostete und für den Einsatz mit SuccessFactors Employee Central optimierte Lösung.

Dann also andersrum: worin unterscheiden sich die beiden Lösungen?

Bezahlmodell: SAP SF ECP ist Teil der SAP SuccessFactors Lösung und wird ebenfalls über Subscriptions bezahlt. SAP HCM On Premise Payroll wird über Einmallizenzen und Wartungsgebühren auf den Lizenzen bezahlt.

Betriebsmodell: Das Payrollsystem von ECP wird durch SAP betrieben. Dies umfasst nicht nur das Hosting (also Anlegen, Bereitstellen und Überwachen des Systems sowie Backup und Disaster Recovery), sondern auch das Einspielen von Patches und LCP. Der Kunde konfiguriert die Lösung nach seinen Bedürfnissen.
Im Fall der SAP On Premise-Lohnabrechnung (auch in der neuen ‘SAP HCM For S/4 HANA’ onPremise Version) ist der Kunde selbst für die Hardware und deren Wartung sowie die Softwarewartung verantwortlich (wobei üblicherweise einzelne oder alle Tätigkeiten an Partner übergeben werden). 

Zukunftssicherheit: SAP verpflichtet sich im Fall der SAP SF ECP gegenüber dem Kunden die Infrastruktur und die benötigte Funktionalität für die Lohnabrechnung zur Verfügung zu stellen. Sollte die Lösung nicht mehr sinnvoll oder nutzbar sein, unternimmt SAP alles Nötige, damit der Kunde seine Lohnabrechnung ohne Unterbruch verarbeiten kann. On Premise Kunden müssen selber für eine Ersatzlösung sorgen, wenn die bisherige Lösung nicht mehr weiter genutzt werden kann und auch die nötigen Umstellungsarbeiten vornehmen. Genau in dieser Situation stecken aktuell die
meisten Kunden. Alle die schon auf das Cloud Modell SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll gewechselt habe, können sich entspannt zurücklehnen: Sie haben ‘eine Lohnabrechnung’ gebucht, was technisch dahintersteht, braucht sie nicht zu kümmern. SAP wählt die am besten geeignete technische Lösung und kümmert sich darum, dass sie läuft

Anpassungsmöglichkeiten: Diese sind beinahe identisch. Etwas genauer bitte? Alles, was man normalerweise benötigt, um die Lohnabrechnung an seine Anforderungen anzupassen, ist es auch in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll möglich: z.B. Pflege von Tabellen, Anlegen von Lohnarten, Rechenregeln, Schemen, Operationen, Funktionen, kundeneigene Programme, Infotpyen und Auswertungen. Nicht erlaubt ist es, SAP Standardcoding anzupassen (was man sowieso nie hätte tun sollen/dürfen) und sogenannte Implicit Enhancements zu aktivieren, was in der Praxis äusserst selten nötig war. Mehr Informationen finden Sie hier: Introduction to Implementing Employee Central Payroll | SAP Help Portal und hier Modification and Extensibility | SAP Help Portal.


Im Zweifelsfall empfehlen wir Ihnen Ihre Lohnabrechnung durch SAP oder Ihren Implementierungspartner dahingehend überprüfen zu lassen.

Payroll Solution Video


Gibt es auch eine Lokale Lohnabrechnung für die Schweiz?

Die Antwort ist klar: Ja. Die Schweiz ist eines von (aktuell) 54 Ländern, für welche eine lokalisierte Lohnabrechnung in SAP SuccessFactors ECP ausgeliefert wird. Stand Q4 2023 rechnen 53 Unternehmen Ihre Belegschaft in der Schweiz über SAP SuccessFactors ECP ab! Auch für die Personalstammdaten (welche im Modul SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central verwaltet werden) gibt es eine lokale Version für die Schweiz.

Der Funktionsumfang in der Schweizer Lohnabrechnung des SF ECP entspricht demjenigen der SAP HCM On Premise Lohnabrechnung. Gesetzliche Anforderungen an Berechnung und Auswertung werden also im Standard abgedeckt und können, wenn nötig an die Situation des Kunden angepasst werden.
 

Mehrfachanstellung in der Schweizer Lohnabrechnung

In einem unserer letzten Beiträge haben wir bereits ausführlich über die speziellen Anforderungen im Zusammenhang mit der Mehrfachanstellung informiert. Auch die Lohnabrechnung von SAP SF ECP unterstützt die Mehrfachanstellung und berechnet alle Versicherungsabzüge korrekt – genauso wie es auch die SAP On Premise Lohnabrechnung macht.
 

Im Standard abgedeckt sind (Auszug aus der langen Funktionsliste):

    • ELM: Zertifiziert 5.0

    • Grenzgänger: Alle nötigen Felder, Berechnung und Abrechnung für DE, IT, FR

    • Quellensteuerberechnung und -Auswertung für alle Kantone mit den unterschiedlichen Berechnungsmethoden

    • Familienzulagenabrechnung für alle Kantone

    • Lohnausweis: gem. ELM 5.0, inkl. Deklaration Lohnnachgenuss

    • Aufenthaltsstatus inkl. der neuen Ausprägungen

    • ELM: inkl. aller neuen Felder zur Ergänzung der AHV-Meldung

    • Alle gesetzlich vorgeschriebenen Sozialversicherungen

    • Freiwillige Zusatzversicherungen wie Krankentaggeld, Krankenpflege und Überobligatorische Unfallversicherung

    • Berechnung der Pensionskassenbeiträge

 

Ich habe gehört, es gibt eine ganz neue, native Cloud Lohnabrechnung. Soll ich nicht darauf warten?

Na ja, wenn sie rund 10 Jahre Zeit haben und bis dahin auf die neusten Technologien verzichten wollen …

Im Ernst: eine native Cloud Payroll wird aktuell entwickelt. Sie ist Anfang 2024 im ersten Land (UK) in das Early Adopter Programm übernommen worden. Die Allgemeine Verfügbarkeit ist üblicherweise ein halbes bis ein Jahr später. Etwa ein Jahr nach UK ist geplant, dass die Lohnabrechnung für die USA in das Early Adopter Programm kommt.
Die Planung für die weiteren Länder steht noch nicht fest. Wahrscheinlich muss mit einem Zeithorizont von 8 – 10 Jahren gerechnet werden, bis die neue Lohnabrechnung für die Schweiz verfügbar wird. Deshalb wohl eher nicht vor dem Ende der bisherigen SAP HCM On Premise Payroll welches 2027 bzw. 2030 mit verlängerter Wartungsperiode) ist. Zudem wird die Lohnabrechnung am Anfang Unternehmen mit eher geringer Mitarbeiterzahl und eher ‘einfacher’ Lohnabrechnungen im Fokus haben.

Wichtig: Alle Informationen zu Inhalt und Verfügbarkeit stehen unter dem üblichen Vorbehalt und sind unverbindlich.
 

Warum sollte ich auf die SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll wechseln?

Die meisten Kunden betreiben heute ihre Payroll immer noch in Ihrem SAP HCM ECC (On Premise) System - häufig seit sehr vielen Jahren und zur vollen Zufriedenheit. Alles ist genauso konfiguriert, wie es sein soll und gesetzliche Anforderungen werden durch die Kunden über die von SAP zur Verfügung gestellten Patches eingespielt und an die Situation beim Kunden eingespielt. Warum soll ich mir dann Gedanken darüber machen, auf die SAP SuccessFactors Lohnabrechnung zu wechseln?

Dafür gibt es drei wichtige Gründe:

1.) End of Life: Nach über 20 Jahren guter Dienste, kommt das bisherige SAP ECC On Premise System an sein Lebensende. Ende 2027 endet die Wartungsfrist. Sie kann (mit einer zusätzlichen Wartungsgebühr von 2%) noch bis Ende 2030 verlängert werden. Dann ist aber endgültig Schluss. Für Kunden, die ihr ERP-System bereits auf das neue S/4 HANA-System umgestellt haben, und das HCM innerhalb des S/4 Hana Systems (im sogenannten Kompatibilitätsmodus) betreiben, kommt das Ende bereits Ende 2025. Dann endet die Nutzungserlaubnis des HCM- (und aller anderen) Kompatibilitätsmodus.

2.) Lohnabrechnung als Prozess und die Möglichkeit, frühzeitig Fehler zu erkennen: Immer wieder hören wir von Kunden, dass die Lohabrechnung sehr flexibel ist und zuverlässig macht, was sie soll. Es fehlt aber die Möglichkeit die Lohnabrechnung als Prozess und nicht nur als einzelne Schritte durchzuführen. Zudem wären die Kunden froh, wenn sich nicht alles auf die Tage während der Lohnverarbeitung konzentrieren würde. Der Wunsch ist, dass bereits im Vorfeld alle Eingaben und Konfigurationen End-to-End bis zur späteren Verbuchung kontrolliert und allfällige Fehler frühzeitig erkannt und durch die verantwortlichen Lohnspezialisten korrigiert werden kann. Genau das ist mit der SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (kurz ECP) umgesetzt und in die HCM-Prozesse in SuccessFactors voll integriert verfügbar
 

Typischer End-To-End Prozessablauf der Gehaltsabrechnung


Hinweis: die Oberfläche, die nötige Konfiguration und die meisten der Funktionen stehen auch den Kunden mit der SAP HCM On Premise Lohnabrechnung zur Verfügung. Stichwort ‘Payroll Control Center’. Startpunkte zu mehr Informationen:

  • SAP HCM On Premise: Payroll Control Center | SAP Help Portal

  • SAP HCM For S/4 HANA: Payroll Control Center (New Payroll Experience) | SAP Help Portal

3.) Reduktion der Kosten durch eine konsequente Umsetzung der Cloud-Strategie: Einzelne oder idealerweise alle Talentprozesse finden in SuccessFactors statt. Dafür zahlt der Kunde eine Nutzungsgebühr (Subscription). Im Gegenzug würden beim Einsatz einer Cloud-Lösung die Kosten für Wartung und Betrieb der Hardware und die jährliche Wartungsgebühr auf den früher einmal gekauften User-Lizenzen wegfallen. Das funktioniert aber nur sehr bedingt, wenn doch noch einzelne HR-Prozesse (wie z.B. die Lohnabrechnung) im SAP HCM stattfinden. Am Schluss zahlt der Kunde zwei Systemlandschaften. In der Folge kann die erwartete Einsparung nicht realisiert werden.
Zudem werden durch die hohe Automatisierung im Betrieb der SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll die Betriebskosten der Payroll drastisch gesenkt.

 

Migration von SAP HCM On Premise Payroll zu SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll:

Grundsätzlich bieten sich drei Migrationspfade an:

  • Greenfield Ansatz

  • (Selektive) Übernahme mit SAP Standard Tools

  • Lift & Shift mit Partner Tools


Greenfield Ansatz

Die Transformation auf SuccessFactors ist immer auch eine grosse Chance zur Vereinfachung und Standardisierung. Dieser Ansatz macht besonders Sinn, falls sich über die Jahre ein Wildwuchs im Customizing entstanden ist, Prozesse sich vom Standard entfernt haben oder die Wartung der Payroll unnötig aufwändig ist.
Es werden nur Stammdaten und das Lohnkonto übernommen, während das Customizing möglichst schlank neu aufgebaut wird.

(Selektive) Übernahme mit SAP Standard Tools

Mit diesem Ansatz werden Customizing und Kundenerweiterungen gezielt über Transporte in die ECP übernommen. Üblicherweise handelt es sich nicht um eine 1:1 Migration, sondern um ein selektives Vorgehen. So können verschiedenste Regelungen z.B. das Abrechnungsschema oder Kundeneigene Infotypen etc. weiterverwendet werden.
Bei diesem Ansatz ist die Komplexität und der Aufwand für Migration, Nacharbeiten und Tests am höchsten. In allen Projektphasen ist äusserste Sorgfalt, sowie ein hoher Einsatz qualifizierter Ressourcen und Experten nötig.
Bei einem generellen Greenfield Ansatz kann ebenfalls selektiv auf die Möglichkeit der Übernahme mit Transporten zurückgegriffen werden. Dies führt zu einer ausgewogenen Mischung aus bewahren und erneuern. 


Lift & Shift mit Partner Tools

Über viele Jahre haben sich Partner Tools mit dem Lift & Shift Ansatz bewährt. Sei es bei Carve outs, Migrationen von SAP HCM zu SAP HCM oder eben beim Wechsel von SAP HCM auf die ECP. Die Tools der Partner extrahieren zielgerichtet Customizing und Daten aus ihrem aktuellen System und übertragen Sie auf Ihre neue ECP-Instanz. Dazugehörende Funktionalitäten zum Monitoring, zur Conversion und zum Datenvergleich runden das Bild ab.
Die Wahl eines bewährten Produkt reduziert die Aufwände und Risiken im Vergleich zum Vorgehen über Transporte erheblich. Bekannte und erprobte Migrationstools gibt es ua. von:

EPI-USE

TIK Nexus

Spinifex IT


Hintergründe und Gedanken zur Migration finden Sie im Implementation Design Principle Dokument IDP - Employee Central Payroll Migrating from ERP HCM Payroll to Employee Central Payroll V1.7.pdf (sap.com)

Und übrigens: Es gibt noch viele andere spannende Implementation Design Principles:
Implementation Design Principles | SAP SuccessFactors | SAP Community

 
Hier geht es zum ersten Teil unserer Serie: Lohnabrechnung mit SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll für Schweizer Kunden

entry.title: Lohnabrechnung mit SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll für Schweizer Kunden
entry.content:
In dieser dreiteiligen Serie beschäftigen sich mein Kollege @RolandChristen und ich mit den Payroll Optionen die sich Schweizer SAP Kunden bieten.

Seit gut dreissig Jahren rechnen hunderte von Schweizer Firmen jeden Monat pünktlich und zuverlässig die Löhne ihrer Mitarbeiter über die SAP HCM Lohnabrechnung ab. Aber die Zeit für den Abschied von dieser bewährten Lösung naht. In wenigen Jahren endet die Lebenszeit des SAP ERP Systems, zu welchem auch HCM und damit die Lohnabrechnung gehört. 

Unabhängig davon ist das System aber auch in die Jahre gekommen. Neue Funktionalitäten und Technologien können den Kunden auf dieser Basis nicht mehr zur Verfügung gestellt werden. Dazu kommt, dass auch Business Anwendungen vermehrt in der Cloud betrieben werden. Dies gilt insbesondere für die Talentprozesse, wo diese Entwicklung schon sehr weit fortgeschritten ist.

SAP deckt diese Bedürfnisse mit der ‘SAP SuccessFactors Suite’ ab. Aber auch im Kernteil des HCM inklusive der Lohnabrechnung wächst das Bedürfnis nach einer modernen Lösung, SAP SuccessFactors hat auch hier mit der SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central und der SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll  die passende Lösung: modern und gleichzeitig sehr bewährt, standardisiert und trotzdem flexibel durch den Kunden an seine Bedürfnisse anpassbar. 

Wie das geht? Wir zeigen ihnen in dieser Serie auf warum SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll für die Schweiz alles Wesentliche so gut wie die bisherige SAP On Premise Lohnabrechnung kann (manches sogar besser), welche Alternativen für die Lohnabrechnung von SAP sonst noch angeboten werden und was bei einer Migration von der jetzigen SAP On Premise Lohnabrechnung auf die SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll zu beachten ist.

Nach einem kurzen Überblick zur SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll in diesem Blogbeitrag, finden Sie Informationen zu verschiedenen Aspekten im Zusammenhang mit der Lohnabrechnung von SAP im zweiten Teil der Serie. Wer sich gerne mittels Bilder informiert, kommt im dritten Teil auf seine Kosten, wo die wichtigsten Kennzeichen und Vorteil von SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll sowie des Payroll Control Center erklärt und gezeigt werden.

 

Überblick SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll ist ein globales Lohn- und Gehaltsabrechnungssystem, das die Lohn- und Gehaltsabrechnungsprozesse vereinfacht und rationalisiert und gleichzeitig die Einhaltung von Vorschriften gewährleistet. Diese Cloud-basierte Lösung wird von zahlreichen Branchen auf der ganzen Welt eingesetzt. In diesem Artikel konzentrieren wir uns auf die Implementierung und die Vorteile der SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll für Kunden in der Schweiz.

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll ist ein Teil der SAP SuccessFactors HCM Suite und eine Kombination aus bewährter Technologie, eingebetteter Lokalisierung und Standard-Lohnabrechnungsprozessen. Die Lösung steht für ein zuverlässiges und genaues Lohn- und Gehaltsabrechnungssystem, das darauf ausgelegt ist, Risiken zu reduzieren, die Lohn- und Gehaltsabrechnung zu vereinfachen und eine intuitive Benutzerführung zu ermöglichen.

 

Was zeichnet die SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll aus?

Was zeichnet die SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll aus?


 Einsatz in der Schweiz

Unternehmen in der Schweiz haben komplexe Regularien und eine vielfältige Belegschaft. Die Verwaltung der Lohn- und Gehaltsabrechnung in einem so vielfältigen und komplexen Umfeld erfordert ein robustes, effizientes und transparentes System, wie es SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll bietet.

    • Sicherstellung der Compliance: In der Schweiz müssen alle Organisationen verschiedene lokale, kantonale und eidgenössische Vorschriften einhalten. SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll verfügt über integrierte Funktionen zur Einhaltung von Gesetzen und Vorschriften, die regelmässig aktualisiert werden, um Gesetzesänderungen zu berücksichtigen. Dies hilft den Unternehmen, die Vorschriften einzuhalten, ohne zusätzliche Zeit und Ressourcen zu investieren.
    • Rationalisierung von Prozessen: Die Lösung bietet standardisierte Lohnabrechnungsprozesse, die es Schweizer Unternehmen ermöglichen, ihre Lohnabrechnungsvorgänge zu automatisieren und zu rationalisieren. Dadurch werden manuelle, zeitaufwändige Aufgaben reduziert, so dass sich die Mitarbeiter der Personalabteilung auf strategischere Tätigkeiten konzentrieren können.
    • Genauigkeit und Transparenz: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll erhöht die Genauigkeit, indem es Fehler bei der Dateneingabe eliminiert und eine Datenvalidierung in Echtzeit ermöglicht. Darüber hinaus bietet es Transparenz mit detaillierten Lohnabrechnungsprotokollen und Prüfprotokollen, die den Unternehmen in der Schweiz dabei helfen, ihre Rechenschaftspflicht zu erfüllen.
    • Mitarbeiter-Self-Services: Diese Funktion gibt den Mitarbeitern beispielsweise die Möglichkeit, auf ihre Lohnabrechnungsformulare zuzugreifen sowie ihre Daten anzusehen und – sofern sie die Berechtigung dazu haben, zu aktualisieren. Das spart nicht nur Zeit für die Personalabteilung, sondern fördert auch eine Kultur der Transparenz und Eigenverantwortung.
    • Integrationsfähigkeiten: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll lässt sich nahtlos mit anderen SAP-Modulen und Drittsystemen integrieren. Dies erhöht die Datengenauigkeit und reduziert die Notwendigkeit des manuellen Datentransfers zwischen den Systemen, was für Organisationen in der Schweiz, die mehrere Systeme zur Verwaltung ihrer Abläufe nutzen, von entscheidender Bedeutung ist.

 

Fazit

Zusammenfassend lässt sich sagen, dass SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll ein leistungsfähiges Tool ist, das die besonderen Anforderungen und Herausforderungen der Unternehmen in der Schweiz erfüllt. Es bietet eine umfassende, skalierbare und effiziente Lösung für die Verwaltung der Gehaltsabrechnung, die Sicherstellung der Compliance und die Förderung von Transparenz und Mitarbeiterengagement. Im Zuge der fortschreitenden Digitalisierung werden Tools wie SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll eine entscheidende Rolle bei der Umsetzung dieser Transformation spielen.

Hier geht es zum zweiten Teil der Serie: Deep Dive - SAP Payroll Lösungen für die Schweiz

entry.title: News from SAP Activate SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402
entry.content:
We are pleased to announce the availability of the updates to SAP Activate. This release brings significant enhancements to the ready-to-run business processes, methodology guidance, and the tools utilized by our customers in deploying their solutions. These updates aim to streamline and improve the implementation process, providing a more seamless experience for our valued users. Here are key highlights for this release, you can learn more in the detailed sections below:

  • SAP Activate Community Blogs
  • SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (3-system landscape and 2-system landscape)
  • Updates to SAP Activate Methodology for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • NEW SAP Activate Methodology for SAP Concur roadmap
  • SAP S/4HANA Test Automation Tool – New features with 2402 release
  • Updates to Activate Content Library (ACL) and Roadmap Viewer

 

SAP Activate Community | SAP Activate Blogs

Join the SAP Activate Community to access pertinent content and resources, and to engage with SAP partners, clients and internal colleagues by posting and answering questions.

Having transitioned from our Community YouTube channel, our SAP Activate minute videos are now available on SAP Media Share. These short videos offer convenient access to updates, accelerators, and solutions for potential project challenges. Explore the playlist for SAP Activate minute video content.

Make sure to follow the SAP Activate tag, where you can discover blog posts like the ones mentioned here, along with additional insights shared by SAP Activate experts within the community.

Recap of some blog posts from the community in Q3/Q4:

  • Transitioning from an SAP ERP – S/4HANA On-Premise Practitioner to an SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition Practitioner – A Guide for Skill Conversion by Marco Valencia
  • Enhanced Microsoft Teams Integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition by Kiran Bose
  • SAP Cloud ALM – Now with new SAP Business Technology Platform relevant capabilitiesby Kiran Bose
  • Activate Roadmap Viewer new features by Jitendra Bhandari
  • Cloud ERP Close-Up: Tooling for ERP as a Service Test Automation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition by Bert Schulze
  • Exploring the New Organizational Change Management Content in SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer by Christina Galbreath
  • Two Key Types of Governance Bodies for your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Project Success by Jan Musil

 

SAP Activate for SAP S/HANA Cloud Public Edition (3SL and 2SL) in 2402

Both the SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (3-system landscape) and SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Cloud (2-system landscape) implementation roadmaps have been enhanced based on product management input, system functionality changes, and customer feedback. Below are highlights for this release.

  • Released a new roadmap SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (3-system landscape) – Early Adopters targeted at our early adopter customers that are using parallel Project Line and the Configuration Localization Tool. This shares content with the SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (3-system landscape) roadmap, but also includes the changes for the early adopters. Users can easily access the new EA content by using the tags Localization and Parallel Project Line.
  • Added the tag Organizational Change Management and expanded the organizational change management content to include the following deliverables:
    • Change Concept
    • Change Plan
    • Stakeholder Analysis
    • Communication Analysis and Plan
    • Communication Plan Execution
    • Detailed Change Impact Analysis
  • Reviewed all tasks for extraneous accelerator assignments and simplified the user experience by eliminating redundant assignments.
  • Improved the guidance related to tax processing. This includes a new task Review Tax Processing Solution in Prepare and updates to the Conduct Finance Workshop and Document Localization Requirements tasks.
  • Increased the visibility of SAP Central Business Configuration action by adding procedure steps to tasks Verify Configuration for Fit-to-Standard Workshop for SAP S/4HANA Cloudand Configure SAP S/4HANA Cloud.
  • Highlighted the deliverables and tasks related to rollout project by adding a new tag 2ndWave Project/Rollout Project.
  • Updates to the SAP Activate for Upgrade of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (3-system landscape) including lessons learned on regression testing and transport management during the upgrade period and business content deployment after the software upgrade.

 

Updates of SAP Activate Methodology for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

The SAP Activate Methodology content for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition has been enhanced with improvements to help guide the customer through the implementation as well as system conversion journey. There have been several updates to User Experience as well as the structure and content for System Conversion in which customers and partners can benefit in the recent releases.

The content is available in the SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer and SAP Cloud ALM.

Key highlights include:

  • In coordination with the Regional Implementation Group (RIG), several updates have been made in both the Discover and Prepare phases in the roadmap pertaining to System Conversion.
  • Significant effort has been dedicated to aligning the roadmap with SAP Clean Core principles and Fit-to-Standard, especially in the deliverables of Technical Project Preparation and Fit-to-Standard Preparation For Specific Features.
  • The User Experience Strategy and Workshops deliverable has been updated both in tasks, procedure and accelerators.
  • Several deliverables and tasks were updated to reflect distinctions between New Implementation and System Conversion.

The updated content can be found in the SAP Activate Methodology for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition here. See the release notes for a complete list of al improvements.

 

SAP Activate Methodology for SAP Concur

Now live – SAP Activate Methodology for SAP Concur. This comprehensive guide is meticulously designed to assist in seamlessly navigating the implementation and optimization journey for SAP Concur. Whether you are a seasoned user or a newcomer, this roadmap is tailored to guarantee a streamlined and efficient process.

 

SAP S/4HANA Test Automation Tool 2402 Release

The primary emphasis on the 2402 release was on providing enhanced and integrated tools to offer our customers a smoother regression testing experience. Some of the highlights include the following:

  • Launch of the Intelligent Test Scope (i-TS) application. i-TS provides customers an optimized regression test scope based on the code changes done in a particular release/update. The optimized scope can be used to run a more efficient Post Upgrade Test (PUT).
  • ABAP Units created as part of the extensibility capabilities of SAP S/4HANA Cloud can also be added to the scope of PUT and made part of the regression scope.
  • Analyze Automated Test Results (AATR) app now shows clearly if the automated failure category and recommendations are coming from Regex Patterns or ML Text Classification.

For additional information, please refer to the release blog for the 2402 update.

 

SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer Updates

Numerous improvements have been implemented to enrich the functionality SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer. These updates encompass a range of improvements designed to enhance user experience and efficiency. Some of the key updates include:

  • Filter by Roles: Roles filters has been incorporated into each roadmap. Users have the option to choose a specific role filter from the left panel of the roadmap, yielding the corresponding results on the right panel.
  • Content condensing: The content is now condensed to enable users to view more data on a single page. The difference becomes evident when a user selects a roadmap and clicks on the content tab.

You can find a list of the updated features in the blog here.

 

Stay tuned for more updates and blogs.

Follow the SAP Activate page on the SAP Community. Remember to turn on your notification to receive updates or information about items requiring your attention. 



SAP Activate Minute

Tune into the SAP Activate Minute videos in our Playlist here. Subscribe to get notified when we drop new videos. We have big plans to add interesting new topics.

Run simple – SAP Activate – Accelerate and simplify the adoption of SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

entry.title: Natural language queries to SAP S/4HANA - Generative AI extension on SAP BTP
entry.content:
Introduction

In the realm of business intelligence, the ability to quickly and effectively access data is paramount. Yet, for many organizations, the process of data retrieval remains mired in complexity, often requiring specialized knowledge of database languages and IT intervention. This creates a bottleneck that can impede the flow of information and the agility of decision-making. This extension prototype presents a transformative solution: a system that allows users to interact with their business data through natural language.

By simplifying access to SAP S/4HANA's rich data landscape, it's not only democratizing data but also enabling a more dynamic and informed business environment.

This blog demonstrates how you can build your own S/4HANA extensions using SAP BTP and Generative AI Hub with intelligent solutions: Simple and secure and for any language!

 

Prototype in action

This video is taken from the prototype application running on SAP BTP Kyma using the Generative AI Hub. It connects to a S/4HANA 2022 FPS02 sandbox system, hence the data we query here is past "Best Practice" data.

Updated video Link: https://sapvideo.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/?entry_id=1_2fwb2mwz 

Since it accesses CDS View Entities, the data retrieved is real-time data. In our case I've added 2 CDS view entities into a schema which of course can be done as needed for your extension use case.

 

Architecture and technology

The prototype uses the ODBC for ABAP driver which is a magnificent tool to safely get data out of SAP S/4HANA. I recommend to read the blogs mentioned in the references section. In the backend there's a Python application running on SAP BTP Kyma secured by XSUAA and the SAP Approuter.

For the purpose of this blog I've written a plugin to SQLAlchemy to reflect the ODBC for ABAP driver specifics. If you are interested to use this library or collaborate on it, contact me.

The prototype setup uses GPT-4 as LLM but I was able to make a few queries work instantly with Codellama 13b. It will likely perform better with a larger Codellama and improved prompting.

 Architecture outline for SQL generation through generative AI models

 

Conclusion

By harnessing the power of the SAP BTP Generative AI Hub, we have unlocked a realm where natural language bridges the gap between complex databases and the insightful queries of business users. This prototype demonstrates that through SAP BTP extensions you can enhance your S/4HANA system, both on-premise and in the cloud, too!

As we stand at the cusp of this new era of human/ machine interaction , it is imperative to recognize the transformative potential that such technologies hold.

 

Call to Action

The future of business intelligence is here, and it speaks your language. Experience the power of this approach firsthand and witness the impact it can have on your organization. Embrace the opportunity to democratize data access, accelerate insight discovery, and safeguard your sensitive information—all within the robust framework of SAP BTP services.

Take the first step towards a smarter, more agile approach to data analysis. Explore the SAP BTP Generative AI Hub and the myriad of services designed to build side-by-side extensions with SAP S/4HANA. Join the vanguard of businesses that are not just adapting to change but driving it. Visit our website, reach out to our team, and begin your journey with SAP BTP today. Your data is speaking; it's time to listen!

References

Using the ODBC driver for ABAP on Linux
Tutorial: Using ODBC driver for SQL Service 

Retrieval Augmented Generation with GenAI on SAP BTP
